resources.name: Energy
resources.description: The lifeblood of modern infrastructure. Must be generated or stored continuously to prevent system failure.

resources.name: Humans
resources.description: The driving force of expansion, they need supplies to be able to work. Their survival depends on stable life support and habitability.

resources.name: Alloy
resources.description: An engineered alloy, offering greater strength and durability than raw metal. Standard material for spacecraft.

resources.name: Carbon
resources.description: A versatile element forming the basis of organics and polymers. Essential for life support systems.

resources.name: Carbon Dioxide
resources.description: A dense atmospheric gas produced by industry and respiration, dangerous in excess. Key compound in climate control and terraforming.

resources.name: Chemical Fuel
resources.description: A propellant combining hydrogen with oxygen. Delivers powerful thrust for deep-space travel.

resources.name: Electronics
resources.description: High-grade electronic components required for constructing advanced buildings and modules.

resources.name: Exotic Alloys
resources.description: High-strength materials used in advanced engineering. Expensive to refine but critical for high-performance structures

resources.name: Fissiles
resources.description: Radioactive elements capable of sustaining nuclear fission reactions. Provide immense energy output.

resources.name: Glass
resources.description: A transparent material refined from silicates, essential for habitats and farms. Cheap and abundant, but heavy.

resources.name: Helium-3
resources.description: A rare isotope used in advanced fusion systems. Extremely scarce but invaluable for late-stage power generation.

resources.name: Hydrogen
resources.description: The lightest and most abundant element, used in fuel production. Invaluable for fusion-based propulsion.

resources.name: Metals
resources.description: Refined from ore, metal enables durable construction in hostile environments. Heavy to transport but indispensable.

resources.name: Nitrogen
resources.description: An inert atmospheric buffer gas required to maintain safe pressure in breathable environments.

resources.name: Noble Gas
resources.description: Rare, chemically inert gases used in advanced propulsion systems. Scarce but important for electric propulsion.

resources.name: Oxygen
resources.description: A highly reactive gas essential for human survival. Used for life support and fuel oxidizers.

resources.name: Polymers
resources.description: Lightweight carbon-based materials, critical for efficient spacecraft and life support systems.

resources.name: Rare Metals
resources.description: Scarce and valuable metals used in precision electronics and specialized manufacturing.

resources.name: Silicon
resources.description: A semiconductor refined from common rock. Required for glass, electronics and solar technology

resources.name: Supplies
resources.description: Food, water and air, necessary for sustaining life on spacecraft and objects without a breathable atmosphere.

resources.name: Water
resources.description: The foundation of survival and growth. Used for drinking, farming, industry, and hydrogen extraction.

research.name: Agriculture
research.description: The growth of food and plants outside of Earth presents new challenges to be solved.

research.name: Biotech
research.description: Application of organisms and biology in technological contexts

research.name: Space Farming
research.description: Although small growth experiments have been conducted in 0g, there are still adaptations needed to allow large scale agriculture in extraterrestrial environments. Usage of appropriate aggregate medium, artificial lighting, radiation shielding, and local power sourcing could allow colonies to feed themselves.

research.name: Bioreactors
research.description: Systems for containing biologically active processes that can allow us to utilize many useful organisms in a controlled manner.

research.name: Regolith Adaptation
research.description: Adapting local regolith into soil or aggregate, greatly expands available growth mediums, allowing more crops to be planted.

research.name: Knallgas Bacteria Farms
research.description: Hydrogen-oxidizing bacteria that can use hydrogen, oxygen, and carbon dioxide as nutrition, producing primarily water that can be recycled back into hydrogen and oxygen. Knallgas bacteria farms can provide protein in a space in a resource-efficient way.

research.name: Genetic Engineering
research.description: We can speed up the slow process of selective breeding by directly finding and implanting beneficial genes, improving crop yields, resistances to disease, and many others.

research.name: Aeroponics
research.description: An advanced form of hydroponics, with plant roots being exposed to a mist of nutrient-rich water solution. Growth of food in the absence of soil or aggregate can provide great improvements, particularly in areas of water usage, and nutrition.

research.name: Radiotrophic Fungi
research.description: Fungi capable of using radiation as an energy source have been previously found on sites of nuclear disasters. Using adapted local soil, they could be used as self-growing radiation shielding for planetary habitats.

research.name: Agricultural Complex
research.description: Once terraforming is under way, we can begin farming outside the confines of greenhouses. Such great farms will require a lot more attention and work than their Earth equivalents, but with a bit of adaptation we'll have fields stretching to the horizon, ready to feed our colonies.

research.name: Closed-cycle Algae
research.description: Tanks of algae are utilized to recycle biological waste and CO2 back into usable nutrition and oxygen, creating a closed ecosystem within the ship.

research.name: Biological Machines
research.description: Engineered assemblies of molecules and proteins can be designed to perform tasks at nanoscale.

research.name: Low-G GMO
research.description: Through the use of genetic engineering, we can adapt our crops to better grow in low- or microgravity environments.

research.name: Chemical Propulsion
research.description: Powerful but inefficient, chemical engines powered the first rockets, and remain the main propulsion for launch vehicles.

research.name: Solid Propellant Rockets
research.description: The simplest form of propulsion, a solid fuel is ignited and directed out of the exhaust to catch fire. While high in thrust and simple in design, the length of the burn cannot be controlled and the thruster will keep firing until out of fuel.

research.name: Kerolox
research.description: Liquid oxygen and a form of highly refined kerosene - rocket fuel - provide the best performance without going fully cryogenic.

research.name: Methalox
research.description: Combustion of liquid methane and liquid oxygen provides improved specific impulses over more traditional propellants while being the easiest to store cryogen.

research.name: Hydrolox
unlocks: +20 ComponentExhaustV on Chem, Chemsmall, Chemhelios, Chemorion
research.description: Liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen are combusted, producing best-in-class specific impulses. While harder to store than other fuels, it burns cleanly into water vapor and can be produced from simple water electrolysis.

research.name: Solid Hydrogen
unlocks: +25 ComponentExhaustV on Chem, Chemsmall, Chemhelios, Chemorion
research.description: Highly pressurized and cooled hydrogen can condense into a solid. This increases density and allows to suspend free radical hydrogen within the ice, improving exhaust velocity.

research.name: Polynitrogen Fuel
research.description: Polynitrogens are highly energetic molecules composed entirely of nitrogen atoms. While the exhaust velocity improvements over hydrogen vary, their over 30 times greater density allows much smaller and more compact launch vehicles, reducing the mass of propellant tanks and drag losses.

research.name: Planetary Habitats
research.description: Permanent solution for extraterrestrial bases.

research.name: Colony Construction
research.description: With our experience in construction and habitation on other planets we can finally realize the dream of permanent colonies outside of Earth.

research.name: Regolith Shielding
unlocks: -35 BuildCost on Outpost, Habitat, Dome Habitat, City
research.description: We can greatly reduce the need for material by utilizing local regolith as radiation shielding in our habitats.

research.name: On-Site 3D Printing
research.description: With laser printing robots we can greatly speed up the assembly of ground facilities.

research.name: Spin-Grav Habitation
research.description: By spinning habitat segments, artificial gravity can be created through centrifugal force. Careful tuning of distance and speed of rotation is required, to maintain a balance between strength of simulated gravity and potential nausea from rotation.

research.name: Permanent Orbital Presence
research.description: To support our expansion into space, we need to establish a permanent orbital infrastructure.

research.name: In-Orbit Production
research.description: By constructing our interplanetary vehicles directly in orbit, we're free from the size and weight constraints of our launch vehicles.

research.name: Orbital Gas Extractor
research.description: Orbital mining station for extracting gases from atmospheres, including gas giants.

research.name: Computing
research.description: The study of computer science and construction of processing units.

research.name: Microprocessors
research.description: Miniature processing units, freeing us of the need for enormous computer frames.

research.name: Nanoprocessors
research.description: Further miniaturization of processing units greatly increases processing power, allowing larger amounts of data to be analyzed, and more complex simulations to be run.

research.name: Early QPUs
research.description: Early advancements in the construction of Quantum Processing Units allows experimental usage of new kinds of computing for specialized data processing tasks. However, the practical applications of such remain limited due to the substantial difficulty in producing circuits that can avoid decoherence.

research.name: Machine Learning
research.description: Statistical models capable of studying and extrapolating from data, potentially solving tasks where writing algorithms is unfeasible.

research.name: Neural Networks
research.description: A type of machine learning inspired by the mechanism of biological neurons, Neural Networks map nodes and their connection as an analog of neurons and are shaped by further training on data.

research.name: Basic AI
research.description: As the size of our learning models grows, so do their capabilities. Impressive pattern recognition when trained on controlled samples can be harnessed for unparalleled processing of data.

research.name: Three-Dimensional Integrated Circuits
research.description: As ever smaller circuits run into physical limits of size, further increases in density of processing power can be achieved by expanding existing circuits vertically, into the third dimension.

research.name: Reversible Circuits
research.description: A special kind of computer circuitry operating on reversible logic, that is logic in which all inputs can be determined based on their outputs. Reversibility of the process means that numerous calculations can be done with minimal entropic losses, greatly increasing energy efficiency.

research.name: Quantum Computing
research.description: Practical implementation of quantum mechanical effects into computing greatly increases capacity for data processing and physical simulations. Although quantum computers cannot replace classical computing in numerous tasks, they can greatly exceed them in others, supplementing existing computing methods.

research.name: Advanced AI
research.description: Refinements, iterations, and increased processing power produce even more capable models.

research.name: Electromagnetism
research.description: The study of electricity and magnetism.

research.name: Phased-Arrays
research.description: A set of radio antennas controlled by a computer, capable of being steered in a specific direction without moving the antenna itself.

research.name: Lasers
research.description: Precisely controlled emitters of coherent light.

research.name: Metamaterials
research.description: Composite materials composed in a way that allows them to have properties not normally observed in naturally occurring elements.

research.name: Electric Propulsion
research.description: Highly efficient but low in thrust, electric drives can handle great distances but are limited by their need for power.

research.name: Hall Thruster
research.description: Ions are accelerated outside this thruster through the use of the Hall Current, giving it its name.

research.name: Ion Engine
research.description: Electrostatic acceleration of argon seeded with potassium through a grid of electrodes provides thrust in this engine.

research.name: MPD
research.description: Magnetoplasmadynamic thrusters utilize the Lorentz force to propel ionized propellant through a generated magnetic field, at velocities far greater than achievable in gridded ion thrusters.

research.name: Optimized Self-field
research.description: A more optimized self-field in an MPD can improve its performance.

research.name: Electrodeless Ion Generation
research.description: By removing electrodes utilized in an ion engine, as well as redesigning for certain efficiencies, we can greatly improve its performance without worrying about corrosion.

research.name: Multi-stage Grid
research.description: Additional grid stages of accelerating grids in the engine allow us to increase the exit velocity of ions.

research.name: Observation
research.description: Astronomy, observation, and study of celestial objects

research.name: Improved Optics
research.description: Improvements in the creation of large lenses can help us observe distant objects.

research.name: Radar Astronomy
research.description: Although RADAR had been invented before WW2 and early observations of the moon through it took place soon after the war, it was not until recently that this technique could be reliably utilized to observe and measure bodies within our solar system. By investing in this technology further, we may find out much more about our planetary neighborhood.

research.name: Space Telescopes
research.description: Ground observations are severely limited by interference from our atmosphere. By placing a remote telescope observatory outside of Earth's atmosphere, we can observe things we couldn't before.

research.name: Exoplanet Detection
research.description: By using various techniques that detect variations in a star's characteristics, we can spot and estimate potential exoplanets circling those distant stars.

research.name: Fusion Theory
research.description: The use of nuclear fusion to generate power.

research.name: Magnetic Field Containment
research.description: In our quest to achieve nuclear fusion, one of the greatest problems is containing hot fusion fuel plasma inside a magnetic field, at pressures great enough to allow ignition.

research.name: Magnetic Nozzle
research.description: As we create more powerful engines, the ever-greater temperatures put more stress on the material of our nozzles. While ever-greater cooling could potentially solve the issue, another concept is to do away with a physical nozzle entirely, instead containing the exhaust energies within magnetic fields.

research.name: Fusion Propulsion
research.description: The application of all our fusion knowledge finally lets us harness it to power our spacecraft.

research.name: Improved Plasma Containment
research.description: By working out inefficiencies in our magnetic nozzles we can more completely capture all the energy from fusion reactions, improving engine performance.

research.name: Fuel Injection Optimization
research.description: An important element of fusion reactions is to supply fuel at optimal rate and direction to achieve as high of a burn up as possible. Improved fuel flow into the reactor chamber guarantees better efficiency.

research.name: Magnetic Spin Alignment
research.description: The holy grail of fusion propulsion. By precisely aligning the spin of individual fuel particles, we can control the direction of resulting neutron radiation, turning previously wasted energy into additional thrust. The reduction in required shielding, and additional exhaust energy massively improves performance of our fusion spacecraft.

research.name: Advanced Fusion Propulsion
unlocks: +25 ComponentExhaustV on Fusion, Fusionnike, Fusionsirius, Fusionzeus, Fusionatlas
research.description: Further optimising our engines, we can improve their performance even more.

research.name: Launch Facility
research.description: Launch infrastructure that can substantially help with reaching orbit.

research.name: Launch Pad
research.description: Proper launch infrastructure can make our landings easier and safer.

research.name: Spin-Launch
research.description: A light payload is greatly accelerated in a centrifuge, then boosted to the final orbit by a small chemical engine.

research.name: Magnetic Launch Rails
research.description: Using the technology pioneered by maglev trains, the launch vehicle is accelerated and launched from a track built on a mountain slope.

research.name: Mass Driver
research.description: Magnetic coils accelerate the payload.

research.name: Electromagnetic Catapult
research.description: Larger and more powerful mass driver capable of launching payloads on interplanetary trajectories by itself.

research.name: Space Elevator
research.description: Supermaterial tether reaching synchronous orbit, with an elevator able to traverse the tether and lift payloads straight to orbital height.

research.name: Mass Driver Propulsion
research.description: Crushed regolith is placed within buckets and accelerated out to 10 km/s down a mass driver. The regolith leaves the engine, transferring its momentum, while the bucket is decelerated to be reused.

research.name: Launch Vehicles
research.description: The primary solution for surface-to-orbit transport.

research.name: Aluminium-Ice Rockets
unlocks: +0 Additional build lv for construction module
research.description: Microscopic particles of aluminium suspended in ice. Once ignited, the aluminium reacts with oxygen from the ice, creating heat, while the liberated hydrogen helps with burn efficiency. While not powerful or efficient enough for Earth, this type of rocket can easily be assembled on-site and does not require extensive cooling that typical cryogenic propellants need.

research.name: Modular Rocket Construction
research.description: After some clever design adjustments, we can adapt our tried and tested launch vehicles for local construction without advanced infrastucture - just construction modules and sufficient resources.

research.name: Early Launch Systems
research.description: Early rockets consisted primarily of solid propellant boosters and kerosene/liquid oxygen launch vehicles.

research.name: Superheavy Launch
research.description: To truly reach out into space we need ever greater capacity to lift entire spaceships into orbit. A new, heavier class of launch vehicles can help us reach the stars.

research.name: Improved Stack Assembly
research.description: As spaceflight is moving out of its early experimental phase, it is time to revise our assembly procedures for mass production.

research.name: Reusable Launch Vehicles
research.description: By adding precise guidance software and controllable surfaces to our launch vehicles, we can allow them to perform a powered landing after a successful launch, letting us use each vehicle more than once.

research.name: Nuclear Launch Vehicles
research.description: Construction of new launch vehicles, augmented with nuclear thermal rockets.

research.name: Optimized Heat Shields
research.description: One of the greatest limiters for full launch vehicle recovery is the need for reentry on the upper stages. A better, more durable heat shield can greatly reduce stress on the vehicle.

research.name: Improved Reusable Rockets
research.description: With our expertise in operating them, we can significantly optimize the cost and time required to construct reusable rockets.

research.name: Condor
research.description: Advanced chemical launch vehicle powered by polynitrogen fuel.

research.name: Magpie
research.description: Powerful launch vehicle using gas core nuclear engines.

research.name: Teratorn
research.description: The largest and most powerful launch vehicle devised, the Teratorn can lift thousands of tons and quickly come back to lift another payload.

research.name: Advanced Payload Fairings
unlocks: +25 MaxPayloadOnCurrentObject on Pelican, Magpie, Teratorn, Condor
research.description: Larger spacecraft can carry more cargo, and so our launch vehicles need even more space to deliver it to orbit.

research.name: Life Support
research.description: It is essential to develop technologies that help sustain our astronauts in good health.

research.name: Crewed Flight
research.description: Safe orbital transport of small crews.

research.name: Improved Capsule Construction
unlocks: -40 BuildCost on Crew Compartment, Crew Medium, Crew Large
research.description: The same technologies that have helped our launch vehicles achieve reusability can be used to improve the construction of our crew transports.

research.name: Improved Ration Packing
research.description: As human space travel becomes more regular, standardization in the form of rations provided to our astronauts is needed.

research.name: Robotics
research.description: Construction and application of robotic systems.

research.name: Artificial Hibernation
research.description: Artificially inducing a state of reduced metabolic rate and bodily activity in passengers and unessential crew will significantly reduce the amount of supplies required to sustain ships during long missions.

research.name: STO Transports
research.description: By adopting design principles from recently developed passenger supersonic aircraft, we can create new shuttles capable of transporting a hundred people at a time.

research.name: Improved Atmosphere Recycling
research.description: The Sabatier reaction can be used to extend oxygen reserves, by recycling carbon dioxide back to oxygen, using hydrogen.

research.name: Suspended Animation
research.description: Enhancements in artificial hibernation technology allow putting the body in a state of near-zero biological activity while still keeping subjects alive. This suspended animation allows passengers to be kept unconscious on long missions while consuming near minimal supplies.

research.name: Exoskeletons
research.description: Frame of servomotors, supports, and polymers that augments the user's physical capabilities. They allow workers to do more and tire less.

research.name: Expert Systems
research.description: Specialized Artificial Intelligence systems, equipped with knowledge and logic designed to handle all tasks within their area of expertise.

research.name: Cybernetics
research.description: Integration of man and machine. Robotic limbs and neural interfaces improve the capabilities of our colonists, pushing the boundaries of humanity.

research.name: Advanced Materials
research.description: Advanced Material Science

research.name: Materials
research.description: Material Science

research.name: Atomic Manipulation
research.description: The process of manipulating individual atoms through the use of a Scanning Tunneling Microscope. This can be used to create atomic-scale structures or unique molecules.

research.name: Space-worthy Fibers
research.description: Our first ventures outside the atmosphere have made us realize that to protect astronauts during excursions we need better fibers to create outfits that can survive outer space.

research.name: Space Alloys
research.description: The harsh environment of space requires us to adapt our materials.

research.name: High-Temperature Superconductors
research.description: High-temperature superconductors are a form of superconductor capable of operating above the boiling point of nitrogen, greatly reducing the need for extensive cooling.

research.name: Debris Shields
research.description: A set of spaced protective layers can protect against micrometeor impacts far better and with far less mass than previous solutions.

research.name: Superconducting Magnets
research.description: Utilization of superconductors in electromagnets can greatly improve their performance.

research.name: Circuit Production In Space
research.description: While a state of vacuum in most of the solar system seems to make creating clean rooms easier, the lower gravity and ever-present static charge build-up still make it a significant challenge to produce electronics off-world. Overcoming those challenges will let us move the supply chain further upwell.

research.name: Graphene
research.description: A form of carbon similar to graphite, with incredible properties.

research.name: Vacuum Forging
research.description: Space brings new challenges and new opportunities in forging. By harnessing the natural conditions of space we can create better metals.

research.name: Room-Temperature Superconductors
research.description: The holy grail of superconductivity, Room-Temperature Superconductors can achieve the state of superconductivity at ambient temperatures.

research.name: Aerogels
research.description: Extremely lightweight materials with excellent insulating capacity and strength per unit mass.

research.name: Improved Electronics Production
research.description: Adapting our space production methods further, we can improve the output of our circuit manufacturing.

research.name: Nanotubes
research.description: Graphene arranged into lengths of tubes. While similar, it can provide many properties that graphene cannot.

research.name: Advanced Alloying Techniques
research.description: New challenges bring new needs, and our material science divisions work tirelessly to develop new, better alloys.

research.name: Diamondoids
research.description: Molecules with diamond-like structures can provide far greater material strength and new properties.

research.name: Mega-scale Carbon Allotrope Application
research.description: While new carbon materials have allowed us to revolutionize material science, to complete ambitious megaprojects we dream of, we need to be prepared to apply them on a scale never heard of before.

research.name: Improved Alloy Production
unlocks: +10 ProductionEfficiency on Exotic Alloy Production, Alloy Smelting
research.description: Optimized procedures and equipment increase yields of our factories.

research.name: Mining
research.description: Extraction of resources from celestial objects

research.name: In-Situ Resource Utilization
research.description: We cannot bring everything we need where we're going. We have to be ready to extract what's needed directly from the ground we step on.

research.name: Regolith Sifting
research.description: While rich ores cannot be always found in space, we can always extract valuable elements by sifting through the ever-present regolith.

research.name: Vacuum-Optimized Boreheads
research.description: Better boreheads designed for operation in a vacuum environment.

research.name: Radioactive Isotope Isolation
research.description: Nuclear power is essential for our continued presence in space. We need to be prepared for difficulties of extracting and enriching radioactive elements necessary for it on site.

research.name: Advanced Mining Methods
research.description: Further improvements in mining equipment and procedures increase yields.

research.name: Helium-3 Extraction
research.description: While sadly there aren't any viable ores of precious Helium-3, it can still be found dispersed in certain environments. As we extract other resources, it is important to make sure that traces of this valuable isotope are not lost among the tailings.

research.name: Improved Fissiles Mining
research.description: Better isotope separation facilities lead to a faster mining process.

research.name: Improved Helium-3 Mining
research.description: With our experience with extracting Helium-3 in space, we can improve our mining facilities.

research.name: Automated Fissile Extraction
research.description: With our newfound experience in extracting radioactive isotopes in space, we can upgrade our existing mining modules with the ability to extract fissile materials.

research.name: Nuclear Power
research.description: The use of nuclear fission to generate power.

research.name: High Temperature NTR
research.description: We can increase the performance of our engines by keeping the fuel elements close to the melting point, increasing the power available for thrust.

research.name: Helium Loop Cycle
research.description: An additional heat transfer system utilizing helium can be used to run a power-generating turbine, or to power a turboinductor device, transferring additional power to propellant.

research.name: Nuclear Propulsion
research.description: Nuclear Thermal Rockets utilize the high energies of nuclear reactors to accelerate propellant to far greater speeds than in chemical engines while maintaining good thrust.

research.name: Improved Reactor Vessels
research.description: To handle greater temperatures, new alloys that can handle thermal loads beyond those of known elements need to be researched.

research.name: Molten Fission Systems
research.description: Nuclear reactors operating at temperatures so high that the fuel elements become molten can achieve far greater power outputs.

research.name: Gas Core Fission
research.description: Even greater power densities can be achieved in nuclear reactors, however this requires temperatures high enough to turn fissile elements into gas. Contained within an actively cooled pressure vessel, rapidly fissioning gas gives off tremendous power - as long as it is contained.

research.name: Liquid Core
research.description: Previously our nuclear rockets were limited by the melting point of uranium. With systems designed to handle liquid nuclear fuel, we can reach beyond that barrier and utilize even greater power in our rockets.

research.name: Nuclear Thermal Electric Rocket
research.description: Using a two-stage system of cooling loops and heat engines, we can more efficiently transfer heat to our propellant while also generating substantial power for an electric afterbooster, significantly increasing the efficiency of our nuclear rockets.

research.name: Closed-Cycle Gas Core
research.description: Fissile gas is reacted inside a transparent pressure vessel - a "Nuclear Lightbulb" - emitting an enormous amount of power transferred to hydrogen propellant.

research.name: MPD-Based NTER
unlocks: +50 ComponentExhaustV on Nuke, Nukenter, Nukeprometheus, Nukeariane
research.description: By utilizing Magnetoplasmadynamic technology in place of the arcjet, we can further boost the efficiency of our nuclear engines.

research.name: Advanced Liquid Core
research.description: An actively cooled rotating fuel drum keeps the uranium in contact with hydrogen propellant while preventing losses of fuel through the exhaust, thanks to centrifugal force.

research.name: Geothermal Power Turbine
research.description: Active planetary geology provides thermal gradients that can be utilized to generate power.

research.name: Wind Turbine
research.description: Wind power generators, similar to ones on Earth, designed for vastly different planetary conditions and ease of construction on site.

research.name: Power
research.description: Provides electricity for planetary bases.

research.name: Solar Cell Arrays
research.description: Utilization of the photoelectric effect for power generation, solar cells have become a ubiquitous form of power supply for spacecraft.

research.name: Chemical Power Reactor
research.description: On planets with abundant hydrocarbon atmospheres such as Titan, energy can be produced from chemical reactions of atmospheric compounds. Hydrogenation of acetylene (or if lacking, ammonia) generates sufficient heat to produce net power.

research.name: Remote Power Transfer
research.description: By utilizing coherent microwave beams and rectennas, we can transmit large amounts of electrical power from far away or even through the atmosphere.

research.name: Improved Solar Cells
research.description: Better materials and technologies allow us to greatly increase the power density of solar cells.

research.name: Powersat
research.description: Large solar collectors in orbit, capable of transferring power down to the surface or other spacecraft by the use of microwave beams.

research.name: Advanced Spacecraft
research.description: Dedicated space vehicles for travel between orbits.

research.name: Stratos
research.description: Powerful upper stage capable of independent operation in space, ideal workhorse for the moon and beyond.

research.name: Selene
research.description: Light craft with an electric engine for moving probes and small cargo to distant objects.

research.name: Solar Sails
research.description: Solar Sails operate using nothing but the sun's energy to maneuver through space. Though slow and limited by distance from the sun, they require no fuel or propellant.

research.name: Spacecraft
research.description: Dedicated space vehicles for travel between orbits.

research.name: Hermes
research.description: Dedicated deep space transport, with artificial gravity, life support capacity, and a powerful array of ion engines.

research.name: Optimized Payload Bus
research.description: Lighter, more efficiently shaped bus will allow us to load more cargo in the same sail craft.

research.name: Iris
research.description: A simple probe carrier craft.

research.name: Expanded Cargo Bays 1
research.description: Improvements on our spacecraft designs allow increasing their cargo capacity.

research.name: Prometheus
research.description: The first true nuclear rocket, more than double the efficiency of chemical rockets while still maintaining high thrust

research.name: Reflective Materials
research.description: The propulsive effect of solar sails is achieved by reflection of photons emitted by the sun. Material improvements in reflectivity allow construction of even better sail based spacecraft.

research.name: Expanded Cargo Bays 2
research.description: Improvements on our spacecraft designs allow increasing their cargo capacity.

research.name: Athena
research.description: Advanced electric spacecraft, designed for slow but efficient travel

research.name: Improved Sail Deployment
research.description: A major issue with launching sail-based spacecraft is unfurling the sail without damaging it. Optimal performance is achieved by the thinnest sail possible, which makes them fragile. Better techniques of deploying the sail would make it far easier to construct and launch.

research.name: Hephaistos
research.description: Nuclear thermal electric spacecraft, capable of significantly cutting down travel time between planets

research.name: Plasma Magnet Sail
research.description: The plasma magnet sail replaces huge stretches of fabric with thin loops of superconducting wire that uses the charged particles of the solar wind itself to generate a far larger magnetic sail, enabling it to fly faster and farther than solar sails.

research.name: Expanded Cargo Bays 3
research.description: Improvements on our spacecraft designs allow increasing their cargo capacity.

research.name: Ariane
research.description: Most powerful nuclear thermal spacecraft, powered by a Nuclear Lightbulb" engine."

research.name: Cronos
research.description: Single-Stage-To-Orbit spacecraft powered by a closed-cycle gas core nuclear thermal rocket engine. A set of seven engines lets this colossus lift itself and a kiloton of payload into earth orbit and beyond.

research.name: Nike
research.description: First generation fusion spacecraft, with efficiency unmatched by previous designs.

research.name: Expanded Cargo Bays 4
research.description: Improvements on our spacecraft designs allow increasing their cargo capacity.

research.name: Sirius
research.description: Advanced fusion spacecraft.

research.name: Atlas
research.description: Enormous spacecraft able to move entire asteroids.

research.name: Zeus
research.description: Operating at the limits of theoretical performance of fusion spacecraft, the Zeus can fly farther and faster than any other.

research.name: Interstellar Travel
research.description: We shall lay down the foundations to construct a great craft that will bring us to other stars.

research.name: Terraforming
research.description: Transformation of celestial objects into an Earth-like environment

research.name: Carbon Release
research.description: We can slowly raise the temperature and pressure of our environment by processing and releasing carbon dioxide from local minerals.

research.name: Stellar Mirror
research.description: An orbital installation of highly reflective material, redirecting sunlight onto the surface of a planet to raise its temperature.

research.name: Artificial Magnetosphere
research.description: Huge electromagnets made of superconductors are used to mimic a planet's magnetic field, shielding the surface from harmful radiation.

research.name: Mars Bacteria
research.description: Genetically engineered bacteria, able to survive and photosynthesize in the harsh Mars environment.

research.name: Photosynthetic Microbes
research.description: Genetically engineered bacteria, able to survive and photosynthesize in the harsh Mars environment.

research.name: Albedo Manipulation
research.description: Seeding the poles with black carbon dust can increase the amount of sunlight absorbed by it, melting the carbon dioxide ice sheets.

research.name: Comet Redirection
research.description: By redirecting comets full of volatiles, we can increase atmospheric pressure and the amount of water present on a planet.

spacecraft.name: Iris
spacecraft.description: Simple, disposable low-mass craft for delivering probes and modules to distant objects.

spacecraft.name: Centaur
spacecraft.description: Centaur

spacecraft.name: Argo
spacecraft.description: Large and advanced chemical spacecraft, capable of taking off from planets on its own.

spacecraft.name: Stratos
spacecraft.description: Reusable upper stage, capable of taking off from other planets.

spacecraft.name: Metis
spacecraft.description: Lightweight deep-space exploration craft designed for long-duration travel through carefully planned gravity-assist maneuvers

spacecraft.name: Selene
spacecraft.description: Light craft with an electric engine for moving probes and small cargo to distant objects.

spacecraft.name: Athena
spacecraft.description: Advanced electric spacecraft, designed for slow but efficient travel.

spacecraft.name: Hermes
spacecraft.description: Long-distance interplanetary craft, incapable of landing.

spacecraft.name: Ariane
spacecraft.description: Most powerful nuclear thermal spacecraft, powered by a Nuclear Lightbulb" engine."

spacecraft.name: Hephaistos
spacecraft.description: Nuclear thermal electric spacecraft, capable of significantly cutting down travel time between planets.

spacecraft.name: Prometheus
spacecraft.description: First true nuclear rocket, more than double the efficiency of chemical rockets while still maintaining high thrust.

spacecraft.name: Cronos
spacecraft.description: Single-Stage-To-Orbit spacecraft powered by a closed-cycle gas core nuclear thermal rocket. A set of seven engines lets this colossus lift itself and a kiloton of payload into earth orbit and beyond.

spacecraft.name: Atlas
spacecraft.description: Enormous spacecraft able to move entire asteroids.

spacecraft.name: Zeus
spacecraft.description: Operating at the limits of theoretical performance of fusion spacecraft, the Zeus can fly farther and faster than any other.

spacecraft.name: Sirius
spacecraft.description: Advanced fusion spacecraft.

spacecraft.name: Nike
spacecraft.description: First generation fusion spacecraft, with efficiency unmatched by previous designs.

spacecraft.name: Interstellar Ship
spacecraft.description: Interstellar generation ship, capable of travelling between the stars.

spacecraft.name: Zephyr
spacecraft.description: Plasma Magnet Sail, unlimited by distance or fuel. It can travel anywhere, given enough time.

spacecraft.name: Talos
spacecraft.description: Improved solar sail with higher capacity.

spacecraft.name: Daedalus
spacecraft.description: A simple photon kite sail. Needs no fuel, but limited in range.

spacecraft.name: Orbital Payload Container
spacecraft.description: Container for cargo deployed to orbit.

summary.label: Good
summary.crew: Our crews can live here with minor issues.

summary.label: Marginal
summary.crew: Our crews will struggle to survive here.

summary.label: Hostile
summary.crew: Our crews cannot land here - the object is too hostile.

launch_vehicles.name: Sparrow
launch_vehicles.description: Cheap, simple, single use small Launch Vehicle.

launch_vehicles.name: Al-Ice Rocket
launch_vehicles.description: Aluminium-Ice LV. Low efficiency but easily assembled from local resources.

launch_vehicles.name: Al-Ice Rocket (Heavy)
launch_vehicles.description: Aluminium-Ice LV. Low efficiency but easily assembled from local resources. Larger variant for heavy payloads.

launch_vehicles.name: Hawk
launch_vehicles.description: Old but reliable super heavy launch vehicle.

launch_vehicles.name: Condor
launch_vehicles.description: Advanced chemical launch vehicle powered by polynitrogen fuel.

launch_vehicles.name: Magpie
launch_vehicles.description: Powerful launch vehicle using gas core nuclear engines.

launch_vehicles.name: Teratorn
launch_vehicles.description: The largest and most powerful launch vehicle devised, the Teratorn can lift thousands of tons and quickly come back to lift another payload.

launch_methods.name: Launch Pad
launch_methods.description: Organized launch infrastructure, reduces launch vehicle launch cost by 10%.

launch_methods.name: Rotary Launcher
launch_methods.description: Launches payloads by utilizing extremely high rotary acceleration.

launch_methods.name: Stationary Mass Driver
launch_methods.description: Set of superconducting electromagnetic accelerators, able to launch payloads directly into orbit.

launch_methods.name: Magnetic Launch Rails
launch_methods.description: Long ramp built atop suitable terrain, outfitted with MagLev tracks to accelerate payloads into space.

launch_methods.name: Electromagnetic Catapult
launch_methods.description: Larger and more powerful mass driver capable of launching payloads on interplanetary trajectories by itself.

launch_methods.name: Space Elevator
launch_methods.description: A supermaterial cable stretching from the surface to geostationary orbit. Allows reaching space just by riding an elevator up the cable. Uses an asteroid as a counterweight; once anchored, all resources from the asteroid will become available.

contracts.name: First Orbit
contracts.premise: We're ready for our big launch! To start off, we need to put a brand new space telescope in Low Earth Orbit. It will let us look above and beyond, and scout out suitable locations for future missions.

contracts.name: Explore Luna
contracts.premise: We cannot hope to reach farther, if we don't start in our closest neighbourhood. It's time to send a probe to Earth's companion. Prepare a spacecraft to deliver a probe to Luna, it will allow us to search for resource deposits on the lunar…

contracts.name: Lunar Landing
requirements: Build launch vehicle: Chemical Launch Vehicle (Superlarge)
contracts.premise: It's time for a grand return to the moon. Let's construct our most powerful launch vehicle, and a spacecraft capable of bringing crew to the moon and back. We might not be the first humans there, but we will make sure to be the ones…

contracts.name: Explore Mars
requirements: Build launch vehicle: Chemical Launch Vehicle (Large)
rewards: Next: Preparation for Human Landing on Mars
contracts.premise: Luna was only the beginning - our next stop will be a greater one. The Red Planet awaits somebody brave enough to reach it. Let's examine it up close and see what we might find there.

contracts.name: Gravitational Slingshot
contracts.premise: With clever use of planetary alignments we can slingshot our spacecraft farther than they would reach otherwise. We recommend using a Selene spacecraft loaded with a probe to fly through Jupiter orbit and reach Pluto - a mission that…

contracts.name: Preparation for Human Landing on Mars
rewards: Next: Preparation for Human Landing on Mars 2
contracts.premise: Sending probes to Mars is easy, but sending humans is a lot harder when you know they have to survive and return. We cannot fail this mission. Let's start with establishing facilities necessary for the safe expedition of our crew to the…

contracts.name: Preparation for Human Landing on Mars 2
contracts.prereq: Preparation for Human Landing on Mars
contracts.premise: With the resources ready, we need to construct a self-sufficient shelter and means of energy production for our crew on Mars, so that they can survive and begin operations there.

contracts.name: Humans on Mars *(final)*
contracts.prereq: Preparation for Human Landing on Mars 2Space Dock
contracts.premise: With the preparations made, it's time to send our brave astronauts to be the first to set foot on Mars. Nobody has ever done it before. If we can do it, the entire space sector will change forever.

contracts.name: Life on Mars
contracts.premise: If we wish to inhabit Mars, we need to make sure that the basic necessities - such as food and air - are taken care of. If our people are able to live there, our chances to colonize it will increase drastically.

contracts.name: Mars Colony
contracts.premise: The time has come to colonize Mars. Now that we can provide our population and industry with everything they need, we should begin construction of habitats to house newcomers and their future families.

contracts.name: Mars City
contracts.premise: With an early colony established, we need to look towards expanding our base of operations on Mars. Being self-sufficient is not enough for us - the colony needs to prosper, bring profits, and serve as an example for the future…

contracts.name: Artificial Magnetosphere for Mars
contracts.premise: For Mars to maintain an atmosphere and our colonists to be safe from radiation, we need to create an artificial magnetosphere. A network of field generators around the planet will let us settle and terraform more easily. They will be…

contracts.name: Mars Early Atmosphere
contracts.premise: The first phase of terraforming only requires a thin atmosphere to be established - uncomfortable and unbreathable, but survivable with just an oxygen mask. It will create a warming effect and let some of the most resilient flora and…

contracts.name: Blue Mars
contracts.premise: Bringing enough water to Mars will make it possible for many organisms to thrive and help regulate temperature. Doing it will require a large-scale operation, but with enough persistence and good planning, we'll wipe the name Red Planet…

contracts.name: Deliver water to Mars
contracts.premise: A great way to deliver water to Mars is by pushing water-rich asteroids onto its surface. If we research engines to do it, and find an appropriate asteroid, we'll be able to bring water to the Red Planet.

contracts.name: Mars Atmosphere
contracts.premise: Establishing a full and breathable atmosphere is no easy task, but an essential one if we want to live on Mars comfortably. We should commence the second phase of atmosphere adjustment.

contracts.name: Warming Mars
contracts.premise: Raising temperature will make the surface more habitable, and allow lower temperature gases and liquids to exist on the planet. As we warm Mars, dry ice and trapped water will be released from frozen rocks.

contracts.name: Martian Industry
contracts.premise: Production and processing of metal alloys will allow us to build more on site. Mars' system's diverse resources are perfect to establish a production base which will satisfy our resource needs.

contracts.name: Trading on Other Planets
contracts.premise: If we have an excess of a resource, or perhaps are in need of some more, we can always trade with other space corporations. Make a marketplace offer with our excess alloys on Mars.

contracts.name: Phobos Carbon *(final)*
contracts.premise: Phobos Carbon To supply our Mars colonies we want a steady stream of easily accessible organics. By constructing an unmanned mining outpost on Phobos and setting up a cyclical mission between it and the red planet, we can keep ourselves…

contracts.name: Probing Lutetia
contracts.premise: If we want to establish a solid economy, we need to experiment with other places for resource extraction. Lutetia is one of the closest major asteroids. With the spectral signature of a metallic type asteroid, it has potential to be rich…

contracts.name: Asteroid Mining
contracts.premise: While we have examined the potential of mining asteroids, we still need to establish a permanent mining base on one. Deploy construction equipment to the asteroid belt so that we may begin building mining infrastructure.

contracts.name: Asteroid Colony
contracts.premise: To oversee our mining enterprise, we require a crew of miners and the facilities to house them. This way, we will be able to establish a 24/7 resource extraction and an uninterrupted supply.

contracts.name: Asteroid Supply Chain
contracts.premise: With asteroid mining underway, we still have to complete the last part of the profit chain - deliver it to our customers for sale. A set of cyclical missions bringing rare metals towards Earth will give us a large surplus to sell on the…

contracts.name: Trading on Earth
contracts.premise: Extra resources extracted in space can be sold back on Earth to the World Governments for profit. Rarer and more expensive resources such as Rare Metals, Fissiles, or Helium-3 are particularly good choices, as well as manufactured goods…

contracts.name: Beyond Mars
contracts.premise: Now that Mars is conquered, it is time for us to reach even further, towards the gas giants. The technology to make use of them is within our reach, so let's strike the iron while it's still hot.

contracts.name: Callisto Landing
contracts.premise: Now that we have gone that far, we are able to reach beyond. Callisto is an obvious choice for the first human landing in the Outer System. Let's go there, and make history.

contracts.name: Reaching Saturn
contracts.premise: After reaching Jupiter, Saturn is the obvious next step in our expansion outwards. We will need to plan carefully in order to minimize costs and mitigate risks - it will be no easy task to reach it and start our operations there.

contracts.name: Titan Landing
contracts.premise: Titan is a great place to start, as the only moon of Saturn with an atmosphere, and the only body in the Solar System besides Earth known to have surface bodies of liquid. It is of great interest to astrobiology, and an excellent place for…

contracts.name: Space Dock
contracts.premise: Having to launch our spacecraft from the surface severely limits our designs. A way to construct them directly in orbit will allow for spacecraft specialized exclusively for navigating from orbit to orbit.

contracts.name: Moonbase Alpha
contracts.premise: A moonbase will greatly benefit our exploration efforts as a forward base outside of Earth's deep gravity well. Establishing such a foothold on our satellite will help us to reach further in space.

contracts.name: Research Laboratories
contracts.premise: To improve our research efforts we need to learn more about the worlds we're trying to explore. You can do this by constructing and crewing laboratories on other objects. You can only build 1 on each object, and they provide diminishing…

contracts.name: Lunar Depot *(final)*
contracts.premise: It was once said that once you're in orbit you're halfway to anywhere - this makes it the ideal place for a refueling point for any flights to the outer system. To keep the depot stocked, make sure to set up cyclic flights from a fuel…

contracts.name: Fleet Expansion
contracts.premise: As our population and influence grow, we're reaching the limit of our space flight capabilities. We need to expand our spacecraft fleet to handle more missions in the near future.

contracts.name: Fusion Power
contracts.premise: For many decades, nuclear fusion seemed to be just out of our grasp, promising unlimited energy if we just managed to push through. Finally, we're on the verge of this breakthrough that will power our future.

contracts.name: Improve Launch Methods
contracts.premise: The growing needs of transportation call for better means of it. To further accelerate our spaceflights, a new system of reusable launch infrastructure is needed.

contracts.name: Propellant Depot
contracts.premise: It was once said that once you're in orbit you're halfway to anywhere - this makes it the ideal place for a refueling point for any flights to the outer system. To keep the depot stocked, make sure to set up cyclic flights from a fuel…

contracts.name: Space Elevator
contracts.premise: Space Elevator To construct

contracts.name: Space Laboratories
contracts.premise: To expand our knowledge we need to expand our research. Laboratory complexes on other planets will allow us to study every little detail of these new worlds, and let us understand them better.

contracts.name: Asteroid Base
contracts.premise: Now that we know that asteroids are worthwhile, we need better infrastructure to capitalize on their resources. An extraction and refueling base for our resource carriers is needed to deliver riches mined in the belt.

contracts.name: Asteroid Pulling
contracts.premise: Our most recent engine design will allow us to move entire asteroids at the small price of building the engine itself and many tons of useless regolith. It's time to demonstrate this capability, and we've found a perfect little rock to…

contracts.name: Asteroid Sample
contracts.premise: Strike the rock! We found great potential on Lutetia, so it's time to take advantage of that. Mine out what you can and bring it back to Earth for examination. There's much money to be made if this goes well.

contracts.name: Asteroid Drop
contracts.premise: Now that we have tested our technology, it's time to use it for terraforming. Bring a water-rich asteroid to Mars in a controlled impact.

contracts.name: Avoiding Armageddon
contracts.premise: We have just detected an asteroid on an impact course with Earth! The impact is calculated to happen in several years, and will wipe out all life from our planet. This might be the most dreadful extinction event in history, a death…

contracts.name: Exoplanet Search
contracts.premise: If we are to leave the Solar System, we'd better have a place worth visiting. We need to find a potentially habitable exoplanet to send an interstellar mission to.

contracts.name: First Step to Interstellar
contracts.premise: We are ready to begin humanity's grandest project yet - to travel beyond our solar system and reach new stars. To do so, we'll need to engineer something greater than ever before - a spacecraft capable of travelling interstellar spaces…

contracts.name: Beyond the Solar System
contracts.premise: The great work has begun. It will take riches of many planets and asteroids to construct, but it shall be one of humanity's greatest achievements.

achievements.name: Do Astronauts Dream of Electric Ship
Do Astronauts Dream of Electric Ship
Do Astronauts Dream of Electric Ship
Do Astronauts Dream of Electric Ship
